points.text: Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — MTX240 — Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GIST) — Molecular glue mechanism identified; brings PDE3a and SLFN12 proteins into proximity to form stable complex, driving RNase-mediated apoptosis independent of KIT signaling.

Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — MTX240 — Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GIST) — Molecular glue candidate advancing in pipeline; mechanism stabilizes SLFN12 to induce apoptosis in GIST cells co-expressing PDE3a and SLFN12.

Erasca Inc. (ERAS) faces a securities class action lawsuit following a $2.8 billion market cap loss after Revolution Medicines accused the company of patent infringement regarding its pan-RAS molecular glue candidate ERAS-0015.

Erasca's lead program ERAS-0015, an investigational oral pan-RAS molecular glue for RAS-mutant solid tumors including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, was linked to a patient death one month post-treatment, triggering regulatory and legal scrutiny.
Novartis entered a ~$1.44B strategic collaboration with Orionis to advance molecular glue therapies for multiple targets, as referenced in industry coverage of concurrent oncology deals.

Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — MTX240 — Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumors (GIST) — Molecular glue program defined; induces RNase-mediated apoptosis via PDE3a/SLFN12 complex formation, bypassing KIT signaling dependency.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors — Revolution Medicines issued a legal challenge alleging patent infringement and trade secret misappropriation concerning Erasca's pan-RAS molecular glue, while the company disclosed a patient death following treatment.

Novartis — Orionis Collaboration — Molecular Glue Therapies — ~$1.44B deal to discover and develop small molecule degraders for multiple targets; specific program names and phases not disclosed in source.
Biodexa Pharmaceuticals — Molecular glue development — MTX240 — Clinical stage biopharma advancing PDE3a/SLFN12-targeting molecular glue for GIST; headquarters and R&D located in Cardiff, UK.

Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Stock price dropped 48% to $9.25 amid patent infringement allegations from Revolution Medicines and a reported patient fatality; facing securities class action for alleged misleading IP claims.
Orionis — Molecular glue discovery platform — Undisclosed oncology targets — Secured ~$1.44B collaboration with Novartis to advance small molecule degrader programs.

MTX240 — Mechanism: Molecular glue inducing PDE3a/SLFN12 complex formation — Phase: Clinical stage — Population: GIST cancer cells co-expressing PDE3a and SLFN12 — Comparator: None specified — Endpoints/Results: Stabilizes SLFN12 to drive RNase-mediated apoptosis independent of KIT signaling.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: Investigational — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors (including pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma) — Comparator: Allegedly compared to Revolution Medicines' RMC-6236 (40mg vs 400mg dose cohorts) — Adverse Event: One patient death reported one month post-treatment.

Orionis-Novartis Pipeline — Mechanism: Molecular glue degraders — Phase: Discovery/Preclinical — Population: Multiple oncology targets — Status: Collaboration initiated for small molecule therapy development.

Erasca — Securities litigation — ERAS-0015 — Hagens Berman filed a class action lawsuit on behalf of investors who purchased stock between Jan 14, 2025 and Apr 26, 2026, citing a $2.8 billion market cap loss due to undisclosed IP disputes and patient safety issues.
Novartis / Orionis — ~$1.44B strategic collaboration — Molecular glue therapies — Includes upfront, milestone, and royalty payments for advancing small molecule degraders against multiple targets.
